description_long: This object is a small, house-shaped public book exchange box, often known as a 'Little Free Library.' It features a pitched, corrugated roof painted white with a pink trim, and the main body is painted yellow with decorative hand-painted text and illustrations. The front has a transparent windowed door with a latch, allowing users to see and access the contents inside. The box is mounted on a single wooden post with a square black base for stability. Various sides display hand-painted messages in Portuguese, including instructions and encouragements to read, as well as sponsor or project labels. The overall design is inviting and playful, intended to attract passersby to interact with it.
description: A small, house-shaped public book exchange box with a windowed front door, yellow and pink paint, and hand-painted messages, mounted on a wooden post.
description_view_0: Front view showing the transparent windowed door with a latch, pink trim, and the label 'Casa das Palavras' above the door.
description_view_1: Side view displaying a yellow panel with the hand-painted message 'LER FAZ a VIDA FICAR mais DOCE' and the white corrugated roof.
description_view_2: Rear view showing a yellow panel with the hand-painted instruction 'PEGUE UM LIVRO! DEIXE UM LIVRO!' and the wooden post.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with sponsor and project labels, a white roof, and a yellow side panel.
attribute_identification_markings: LER FAZ a VIDA FICAR mais DOCE
PEGUE UM LIVRO! DEIXE UM LIVRO!
